meaning: adj.训练的,规律的,训育的n.训练者,规律,厉行纪律的人
examples: He has a reputation for being a strict disciplinarian.
She has some old-fashioned values and can be a strict disciplinarian.
Under the pressures of top-flight management, Graham's public persona changed from good-humoured charmer to dour disciplinarian.
During any given day, she may serve as instructor, mentor, counselor, intermediary, motivator, nurse, and disciplinarian.
